seoTitle: Create a Financial Board in Monday.com (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Build a financial tracking board in Monday.com with formula-driven margins, payment alerts, and a visual dashboard for cash flow.
intro: Cluttered spreadsheets make it hard to know where your business actually stands financially. This guide shows how to build a financial tracking board in Monday.com, structuring income and expenses, automating margin calculations, and setting up alerts for overdue invoices. You'll end with a visual dashboard that gives you cash flow insight at a glance.

steps.heading: Create your financial board
steps.body: Set up groups like Accounts Receivable, Accounts Payable, and Fixed Costs to organize your finances.

steps.heading: Add transaction tracking columns
steps.body: Use Numbers, Formula, Status, and Date columns to record exact values, tax calculations, payment status, and due dates.

steps.heading: Configure formula calculations
steps.body: Set up a Formula column to calculate real-time net margins, tax liabilities, or budget variances automatically.

steps.heading: Automate overdue invoice alerts
steps.body: Build automations to notify accounting teams when an invoice status shifts to "Overdue" or a budget cap is reached.

steps.heading: Build a visual dashboard
steps.body: Switch to Chart View and Dashboard Layout to monitor cash flow trends, monthly spend, and total revenue.

steps.heading: Secure sensitive financial rows
steps.body: Set column-level and board-level permissions to keep sensitive financial data restricted to the right people.

faq.question: Can Monday.com calculate my net margins automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, the Formula column can calculate real-time net margins, tax liabilities, or budget variances as data changes.

faq.question: Will I be alerted about overdue invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure automations to notify accounting teams the moment an invoice status shifts to "Overdue."

faq.question: How do I keep financial data private from the rest of the team?
faq.answer: Column-level and board-level permissions let you restrict access to sensitive financial rows.

faq.question: Can I visualize spending trends over time?
faq.answer: Yes, the Chart View and Dashboard Layout let you monitor cash flow trends and monthly spend distributions visually.
